nodes.name: 淡足鸌
nodes.summary: 淡足鸌（Flesh-footed shearwater，学名 Ardenna carneipes），在图谱中标记为近危（NT）物种，主要分布于约翰·古尔德、太平洋、豪勳爵島，主要威胁包括气候变化、污染。

nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: 污染
nodes.summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。

nodes.name: 豪勳爵島
nodes.summary: 豪勳爵島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联11种鸟类，包括红嘴热带鸟、红尾热带鸟、Kermadec petrel等11种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

nodes.name: 约翰·古尔德
nodes.summary: 约翰·古尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联71种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、凍原豆雁、黄腹角雉等71种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 太平洋
nodes.summary: 太平洋是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联49种鸟类，包括针尾鸭、马岛冢雉、斑肩姬地鸠等49种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The flesh-footed shearwater (Ardenna carneipes) is a medium-large shearwater that mainly inhabits the Indo-Pacific

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | date=2022 | title=Palaeoceanographic changes in the late Pliocene promoted rapid diversification in pelagic seabirds | journal=Journal of Biogeography | volume=49 | issue=1 | pages=171–188 | doi=10.1111/jbi.14291 | doi-access=free | hdl=2445/193747 | hdl-access=free }} The flesh-footed shearwater breeds in colonies, and has two main breeding areas; one in the southwest Pacific Ocean includes Lord Howe Island (22,654 pairs ), South Australia (about 1,800 pairs breeding on two islandsLavers J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | date=2022 | title=Palaeoceanographic changes in the late Pliocene promoted rapid diversification in pelagic seabirds | journal=Journal of Biogeography | volume=49 | issue=1 | pages=171–188 | doi=10.1111/jbi.14291 | doi-access=free | hdl=2445/193747 | hdl-access=free }} The flesh-footed shearwater breeds in colonies, and has two main breeding areas; one in the southwest Pacific Ocean includes Lord Howe Island (22,654 pairs ), South Australia (about 1,800 pairs breeding on two islandsLavers J

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Marine Ecology Progress Series 491:265-275 The species also suffers from climate related impacts Bond AL, Lavers JL (2014) Climate change alters the trophic niche of a declining apex marine predator

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Global Change Biology 20: 2100-2107 and significant heavy metal contamination, the cause of which is not fully understood, but is likely due to the ingestion of significant quantities of plastic, which the birds mistake for food floating on the ocean surface.Bond AL, Lavers JL (2011) Trace element concentrations in feathers of Flesh-footed Shearwaters (Puffinus carneipes) from across their breeding range
